communicative_purpose: To propose getting together, connecting, or spending time with someone
trigger_situations: Planning to meet friends, proposing hangouts, reconnecting with someone
contexts: Social media DMs, texting, casual invitations, rekindling friendships
variations: on se capte, on se fait une soirée, on se voit, on se retrouve
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'on se capture' (false friend); overly casual in professional contexts
collocates: un de ces quatre, ce soir, ce week-end, quand tu veux
example_1: Ça fait longtemps ! Il faut qu'on se capte ce week-end.
example_2: On se capte ce soir pour le cinéma ? J'ai les tickets.
example_3: T'as vu Sophie ? Il faut qu'on se capte, ça nous manque.
example_1_translation: It's been a long time ! We need to catch up this weekend.
example_2_translation: Shall we catch up tonight for the cinema? I have the tickets.
example_3_translation: Have you seen Sophie? We need to understand, we miss it.

communicative_purpose: To enthusiastically agree to do something
trigger_situations: Accepting invitations, confirming plans, showing enthusiasm
contexts: Making plans, party invitations, activities with friends
variations: je suis chaud, je suis chaude, je suis partant, je suis OK, ça me dit
common_mistakes: Gender agreement with 'chaude'; confusing with sexual meaning in some contexts
example_1: Tu veux aller à la plage demain ? Je suis chaud !
example_2: Une partie de jeux vidéo ? Je suis chaud grave !
example_3: Je suis chaud pour le concert, mais j'ai pas de ticket.
example_1_translation: Do you want to go to the beach tomorrow? I'm hot!
example_2_translation: A game of video games? I'm seriously hot!
example_3_translation: I'm keen for the concert, but I don't have a ticket.

primary_function: Slang expression of amazement or shock
communicative_purpose: To express that something is incredible, shocking, or impressive
trigger_situations: Reacting to impressive things, surprising news, extreme situations
contexts: Concerts, sports, viral content, incredible stories, disbelief
variations: c'est ouf, c'est oufimum, c'est grave ouf, c'est trop ouf
common_mistakes: Spelling variations (ouf, ouff, ouf); using it ironically without context
example_1: Le concert d'hier soir, c'était ouf ! J'ai tout loved.
example_2: Il a marqué 50 points, c'est ouf ce joueur !
example_3: Cette montagne, c'est oufimum, j'ai jamais vu ça.
example_1_translation: Last night's concert was amazing! I loved everything.
example_2_translation: He scored 50 points, this player is amazing!
example_3_translation: This mountain is amazing, I've never seen it.

meaning: a plan / a spot / a thing to do
communicative_purpose: To refer to plans, activities, or places to hang out
trigger_situations: Making weekend plans, suggesting activities, checking what's happening
contexts: Planning with friends, suggesting hangouts, weekend activities
variations: un plan, un bon plan, un plan cool, un plan nul, tu connais un plan
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'un plan' meaning a map or scheme; plural 'des plans'
collocates: cool, génial, naze, pour ce soir, pour le week-end
example_1: T'as un plan pour ce soir ? On pourrait sortir.
example_2: C'est un bon plan ce restaurant, les prix sont corrects.
example_3: J'ai un plan : on va au cinéma puis on mange un burger.
example_1_translation: Do you have a plan for tonight? We could go out.
example_2_translation: This restaurant is a good deal, the prices are correct.
example_3_translation: I have a plan: we go to the cinema then we eat a burger.

meaning: it's so trendy / it's all the rage
communicative_purpose: To say something is very popular or fashionable right now
trigger_situations: Discussing fashion, trends, popular activities, viral things
contexts: Fashion, social media, teen culture, what's popular
variations: c'est trop la mode, c'est super mode, c'est hype, c'est en vogue
common_mistakes: Using overly formal 'en vogue' in casual teen contexts; mispronunciation
example_1: Les grosses baskets, c'est trop la mode cette saison.
example_2: Ce filtre Instagram, c'est trop la mode sur TikTok.
example_3: Le vintage, c'est vraiment la mode maintenant.
example_1_translation: Chunky sneakers are so fashionable this season.
example_2_translation: This Instagram filter is so fashionable on TikTok.
example_3_translation: Vintage is really in fashion now.

communicative_purpose: To announce leaving a place or situation
trigger_situations: Leaving parties, escaping boring situations, departing from gatherings
contexts: Parties, social events, work, school, awkward situations
variations: je me casse, je me tire, je me barre, je me tire, je pars
common_mistakes: Confusing with literal 'I break myself'; literal translation of English 'I crash'
collocates: de là, du party, de cette réunion, maintenant
example_1: Cette fête est nulle, je me casse.
example_2: Je me casse avant que le boss arrive, à demain !
example_3: On se casse ? J'en peux plus de cette soirée.
example_2_translation: I'll leave before the boss arrives, see you tomorrow!
example_3_translation: Shall we break? I can't take anymore of this evening.

meaning: it's my thing / that's my jam
primary_function: Expression of personal preference or passion
communicative_purpose: To say something is something you love, identify with, or are known for
trigger_situations: Talking about hobbies, personal taste, identity expressions
contexts: Social media bios, friend conversations, personal declarations
variations: c'est mon truc, c'est mon truc, c'est mon kiff, c'est ma came
common_mistakes: Confusing 'truc' and 'truc' meaning different things; overusing in serious contexts
example_1: La cuisine, c'est mon truc. Je suis chef à mes heures.
example_2: Les escape games, c'est mon kiff ! J'en fais tout le temps.
example_3: Les films d'horreur, c'est vraiment mon truc.
example_1_translation: Cooking is my thing. I'm a chef in my own time.
example_2_translation: Escape games are my favorite! I do it all the time.
example_3_translation: Horror films are definitely my thing.

communicative_purpose: To ask where someone is located right now
trigger_situations: Meeting up, coordination, not knowing someone's location
contexts: Texting, phone calls, finding people at events, travel
variations: t'es où, t'es où, tu化合ya, où t'es, où est-ce que tu es
common_mistakes: Spelling inconsistencies with 'où'; confusing with 'tu es' meaning 'you are'
example_1: Yo, t'es où ? Je t'attends devant le café.
example_2: On est perdus, t'êtes où exactement ?
example_3: T'es où en ce moment ? Tu travailles encore ?
example_1_translation: Yo, where are you? I'll wait for you outside the café.
example_2_translation: We're lost, where exactly are you?
example_3_translation: Where are you at the moment? Are you still working?

communicative_purpose: To say something is too extreme, excessive, or overwhelming
trigger_situations: Reacting to extreme situations, too much drama, overwhelming events
contexts: Drama situations, extreme behavior, overwhelming schedules, emotional overload
variations: c'est too much, c'est too much, c'est trop, c'est too much pour moi
common_mistakes: English mixing in formal French; overuse diluting meaning
example_1: Le drama dans cette histoire, c'est too much !
example_2: Cette charge de travail, c'est too much pour moi.
example_3: Ses jokes sont drôles mais parfois c'est too much.
example_1_translation: The drama in this story is too much!
example_2_translation: This workload is too much for me.
example_3_translation: His jokes are funny but sometimes it's too much.

meaning: I'm all in / I'm fully committed
primary_function: Expression of full commitment or enthusiasm
communicative_purpose: To express being completely invested, enthusiastic, or committed
trigger_situations: Showing enthusiasm for projects, activities, relationships
contexts: Sports, hobbies, work projects, relationships, celebrations
variations: je suis à fond, je suis à fond dedans, je suis à block
common_mistakes: Confusing with being physically 'at the bottom'; overusing it
collocates: dedans, sur ce projet, sur le coup, total
example_1: Ce nouveau projet me passionne, j'y suis à fond !
example_2: Elle est à fond dans le yoga en ce moment.
example_3: Je suis à fond pour cette cause, comptez sur moi.
example_1_translation: This new project fascinates me, I'm totally into it!
example_2_translation: She's really into yoga at the moment.
example_3_translation: I'm all for this cause, count on me.

communicative_purpose: To express that something is bad, disappointing, or not worth it
trigger_situations: Disliking something, criticizing activities, expressing disappointment
contexts: Movies, food, events, plans, people, situations
variations: c'est nul, c'est trop nul, c'est naze, c'est mauvais, c'est de la merde (vulgar)
common_mistakes: Using vulgar version in polite company; literal translation issues
collocates: naze, ennuyeux, mauvais, nul, pas bien
example_1: Ce film était vraiment nul, on s'ennuyait.
example_2: C'est nul qu'il pleuve aujourd'hui, le barbecue est compromis.
example_3: Cette appli est nulle, elle plante tout le temps.
example_1_translation: This movie was really bad, we were bored.
example_2_translation: It sucks that it's raining today, the barbecue is compromised.
example_3_translation: This app sucks, it crashes all the time.

meaning: do you have a buddy / do you know someone
communicative_purpose: To ask if someone knows someone who can help or if they have a friend in common
trigger_situations: Looking for connections, networking, asking for favors
contexts: Job hunting, social connections, seeking recommendations, introductions
variations: t'as un poto, tu connais un poto, tu connais quelqu'un, t'as pas un contact
common_mistakes: Using 'poto' (friend) when looking for romantic connections; being too casual for business
collocates: qui peut, pour, qui bosse, dans ce domaine
example_1: T'as un poto qui bosse dans le digital ? Je cherche un stage.
example_2: J'ai besoin d'un devis, tu connais un artisan ? T'as un poto ?
example_3: C'est pour organiser une fête, t'as un poto DJ ?
example_1_translation: Do you have a friend who works in digital? I'm looking for an internship.
example_2_translation: I need a quote, do you know a craftsman? Do you have a friend?
example_3_translation: It's to organize a party, do you have a DJ friend?

communicative_purpose: To propose doing an activity together
trigger_situations: Making plans, suggesting activities, bonding time
contexts: Weekend plans, casual invitations, friend gatherings
variations: on se fait un truc, on se fait ça, on fait un truc, on fait quoi
common_mistakes: Confusing with literal 'we make a thing'; being too vague
example_1: Demain c'est férié, on se fait un truc ?
example_2: On se fait un truc ce week-end, genre brunch ?
example_3: T'as envie qu'on se fasse un truc ce soir ?
example_1_translation: Tomorrow is a holiday, shall we do something?
example_2_translation: Shall we do something this weekend, like brunch?
example_3_translation: Do you want to do something tonight?

primary_function: Slang expression of difficulty or tension
communicative_purpose: To say a situation is difficult, risky, or uncomfortable
trigger_situations: Tense situations, risky decisions, uncomfortable scenarios
contexts: Relationships, risky bets, dangerous situations, social tensions
variations: c'est chaud, c'est tendu, c'est risqué, c'est chaud chaud
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'je suis chaud' (I'm eager); literal translation of 'it's hot'
example_1: Cette histoire avec Julie, c'est chaud entre eux.
example_2: Je sais pas si je dois lui dire, ça va être chaud.
example_3: Le match contre cette équipe, ça va être chaud.
example_1_translation: This story with Julie is hot between them.
example_2_translation: I don't know if I should tell him, it's going to be hot.
example_3_translation: The match against this team is going to be hot.

meaning: like that / sort of like that
communicative_purpose: To make a vague comparison or show something imprecisely
trigger_situations: Explaining something vaguely, showing style, giving approximate descriptions
contexts: Fashion explanations, approximate directions, casual descriptions
variations: genre comme ça, à peu près comme ça, quelque chose comme ça, genre
common_mistakes: Overusing 'genre' until it loses meaning; being too vague
collocates: comme ça, à peu près, approximatif, vaguement
example_1: Je veux un sac genre comme ça, mais moins cher.
example_2: C'est bien genre comme ça ? Je suis pas sûr du style.
example_3: Il est cool genre comme ça, tu vas voir.
example_1_translation: I want a bag like this, but cheaper.
example_2_translation: Is it like that? I'm not sure of the style.
example_3_translation: He's kind of cool like that, you'll see.
